resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a digestive diagnosis, symptom explanation, microbiome result, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, lab interpretation, medication action, or treatment result.
resultRows.description: This local evidence row is included because the fixture supplies a derived marker call for educational lactose-related context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, gut testing, environment, medications, and professional review matter for digestive-health decisions.

resultRows.sourceLabel: MedlinePlus DTC result limits / Nutritional genomics review / GWAS Catalog
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context and observed sample structure, but it should not make IBS, ulcer, reflux, constipation, infection, gluten, lactose, microbiome, probiotic, curcumin, supplement, lab, medication, diagnosis, or treatment conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.item: Health report scoring and recommendation method
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Health report scoring and recommendation method: sample label Risk scores, percentile gauges, and impact/evidence recommendation scores. The sample explains that Health Reports use DNA analysis to display risk scores or gauges, compare the score to a sample population percentile, and rank recommendations by impact and evidence.
resultRows.description: The local SelfDecode Gut Health sample PDF page 4 presents this as an observed report-output row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is an observed SelfDecode Gut Health sample-output row used to preserve report structure. It is not a local result and must not be used as personal digestive diagnosis, microbiome status, symptom explanation, diet, elimination-diet, probiotic, curcumin, supplement, lab, medication, or treatment advice.

gutHealthContext: Use this report as educational gut-health genetics context, not as diagnosis, lab interpretation, microbiome testing, or a care plan.
Treat Gut Health sample rows as examples of report structure unless the local run supplies equivalent validated evidence.
Review symptoms, diet history, gut testing, labs, medications, medical history, and goals with qualified professionals when making digestive-health decisions.

Do not use genotype alone to diagnose digestive disorders, explain symptoms, start elimination diets, take probiotics or curcumin, change supplements, interpret labs, change medication, or guide treatment.

references.title: MedlinePlus Genetics: What DTC genetic test results mean
Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies a derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 call for lactase-persistence context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as educational lactose-related marker context only; do not diagnose lactose intolerance, digestive disease, microbiome status, symptoms, diet needs, supplement needs, lab status, medication action, or treatment from genotype alone.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: calibrated gut-health, digestive-disorder, microbiome, symptom, diet-history, elimination-diet, probiotic, supplement, lab, medication, treatment, and ancestry applicability models unavailable
genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture intentionally omits calibrated Gut Health models and non-genetic context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Mark IBS, ulcers, indigestion, acid reflux, constipation, gallstones, pancreatitis, IBD, appendicitis, infections, gluten sensitivity, lactose-intolerance diagnosis, microbiome status, probiotics, curcumin, supplements, labs, medications, diagnosis, and treatment unavailable instead of inferring them.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: sample IBS top-variant table visible by OCR; exact first genotype partially OCR-noisy
genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Irritable Bowel page shows a top-variants table for the sample typical-likelihood IBS row.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat sample variants as local IBS, symptom, diet, lab, supplement, medication, or treatment evidence.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: sample peptic-ulcer top-variant table visible by OCR
genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Peptic Ulcers page shows a top-variants table for the sample typical-likelihood peptic-ulcer row.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat sample variants as local ulcer, H. pylori, medication, symptom, diagnosis, lab, or treatment evidence.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Probiotics recommendation page links IL23R rs11209026 GG to IBD-related personalization text.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not infer local IBD, probiotic need, gut inflammation, supplement use, diagnosis, or treatment.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Curcumin recommendation page links an NFKB1 variant row to gut-inflammation personalization text.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not infer local gut inflammation, curcumin need, supplement dosing, medication interaction, diagnosis, or treatment.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Curcumin peptic-ulcer page links TNF rs1800630 CA to peptic-ulcer personalization text.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not infer local ulcer risk, inflammation status, curcumin need, supplement dosing, diagnosis, or treatment.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace body/detail extraction has not been captured for Gut Health.

No calibrated gut-health, digestive-disorder, microbiome, symptom, diet-history, elimination-diet, probiotic, supplement, lab, medication, treatment, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.

Broader symptoms, food history, diet history, gut testing, lab results, medication history, medical history, ancestry applicability, environment, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
Sample-only Gut Health rows are not local genotype evidence or personal digestive-health findings.
uncertainty: Digestive symptoms, microbiome status, infections, reflux, ulcers, food intolerance, constipation, IBD, supplement response, and treatment decisions depend on genetics plus non-genetic context and professional review.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose conditions, explain symptoms, prescribe diets, recommend probiotics or curcumin, interpret labs, change medication, or guide treatment.
Gut Health sample labels and recommendations are source-bound examples of report structure and are not calibrated probabilities for this local fixture.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not integrate clinical labs, microbiome testing, symptom history, diet history, medications, or medical history; it does not diagnose digestive disease, prescribe elimination diets, recommend supplements, change medication, or provide treatment guidance.
Authenticated Sequencing.com marketplace body/detail extraction remains pending, so this is sample-backed output structure, not formal-equivalent parity.
